EDITORIAL_BALANCE: Owner directive 2026-07-21: US audience. When the intake offers US domestic stories that clear the sourcing bar, roughly half the ranked list should be US domestic; international still ranks on merit and leads when it is the day's biggest verified story. Enforced in prompts/editor.md (AUDIENCE BALANCE), not as a hard quota.
ELECTION_CALENDAR_NOTE: Owner directive 2026-07-22: results nights are the most predictable misses, so they are staffed by default. Windows are deliberately approximate (they bias the editor's staffing, never facts); refresh each cycle. Results discipline lives in prompts/editor.md (ELECTION NIGHTS).

tagline: Every story, sourced. Every source, rated.

budget.note: Hard fail-closed cap restored to the Haiku economy sizing (cost lockdown 2026-07-21, crypto parity); a typical full run lands ~$0.15-0.30. The top_n 12 volume setting adds only cents at Haiku prices.

researcher.note: Role 1 of the three-role editorial pipeline: builds the structured research brief (data points + confidence labels + the other side of the story) from the fetched source pages. Fact fidelity is the whole job. COST LOCKDOWN (owner directive 2026-07-21): Haiku, crypto-desk parity.
researcher.note_capacity: Output ceilings sized for a full 12-story day (2026-07-22: the researcher hit the 8000 cap on all three ladder rungs once the intake fattened; Haiku 4.5 allows 64k output, and a ceiling only bills when used).

writer.note: Full-length stories (350-650 words) in batches of 3 per call; the cap covers 3 complete drafts with headroom so JSON never truncates. COST LOCKDOWN (owner directive 2026-07-21): Haiku, crypto-desk parity.

approver.note: Role 3: post-draft last line of defense (accuracy/liability -> balance -> quality, categorized rejections). A different call from the writer so the builder never approves their own work.

models.note: COST LOCKDOWN (owner directive 2026-07-21, supersedes the quality-launch A/B): crypto is the priority bet and this desk must never run a higher cost than the crypto desk. claude-haiku-4-5 on every desk stage, exactly like crypto; the deterministic fail-closed gates (source liveness, dedup, depth gate, belts, corroboration rules) are the quality floor, not the model. wraprescue keeps claude-sonnet-5 for the rare edition-failure path only, same as crypto. REVERSAL TRIGGER: real audience traction on this desk -> revisit the writer model.

publicist.note: Publicist DISABLED per strategy 2026-07-21: LinkedIn is currently the government-sales channel. Code kept in the repo, schedule removed from publicist.yml. Re-enable only on explicit direction.

wrap.note: The Daily Edition (Morning Brief / Closing Wrap, wrap.py): synthesis over the desk's own published stories. COST LOCKDOWN (owner directive 2026-07-21): Haiku, crypto-desk parity.

wrapcheck.note: Independent trace check on the edition: every specific fact must come from the provided inputs; no advocacy, no predictions; calm register. Separate call from the writer.

aging.note: Monthly post-publication review (aging.py, report-only): has any published story's premise been invalidated? Conservative flagging; a human decides on corrections.

wraprescue.note: The Daily Edition's second-chance generator: when the Haiku edition fails its trace check, the corrective rewrite runs here.

sources.note: source_tier drives editorial weight. primary = the official public record (courts, central bank, Congress: documents, rulings, and press releases straight from the institution). major = established outlets with editorial standards, DELIBERATELY spread across the political spectrum so the desk never sees one side's telling alone; each domain carries a published bias/factual rating in site/data/credibility.json, shown to readers with attribution. mixed = aggregated or rumor-adjacent feeds (never publish alone). All feeds below are free and keyless, verified live 2026-07-19. AP and Reuters retired their public RSS (license-only now): documented as the paid upgrade path. The honesty layer is the pipeline (tiering, cross-outlet corroboration, adversarial verifier, primary-source linking, transparent outlet ratings), not any single API.

rss.name: U.S. Department of Justice
rss.note: The Supreme Court retired its public RSS (cases.aspx now redirects to PageNotFound, confirmed 2026-07-19); the desk still cites supremecourt.gov documents directly, they just do not arrive by feed.

rss.name: The Hill Floor Action
rss.note: House floor coverage (added 2026-07-23: the $1.15T NDAA was the day's top domestic story and never reached the intake; the general feeds under-cover roll-call votes).

rss.name: NHC Atlantic
rss.note: National Hurricane Center official advisories (added 2026-07-23: Tropical Storm Bertha's Gulf landfall was a top competitor story with no desk coverage). Official-record source: NOAA's own advisories, not journalism.

sources.keywords_note: Optional per-feed 'keywords' gate: high-volume feeds keep only items matching a keyword in headline+snippet, so they cannot flood the editor. Items matching a 'narratives' watchlist entry always pass the gate. The news desk currently runs ungated; the editor's ranking plus the balance spread is the filter. Add gates if any single feed starts flooding intake.

narratives.note: The desk's ongoing-storyline watchlist, maintained by the editor-in-chief. A cluster whose headline/snippet matches any keyword gets tagged with the narrative's name in items.json, the editor treats a genuine development in a tagged narrative as presumptively rank-worthy, and watchlist matches always pass a feed's keyword gate. Seeded for July 2026; add narratives as they emerge, retire them when they resolve.

dedupe.note: Near-identical stories across outlets (same event, many rewrites) collapse into one cluster by normalized-headline token overlap or shared URL. The cluster keeps the highest-tier source as primary and lists the rest as corroboration; for the news desk, corroboration ACROSS the bias spectrum is what earns top billing.

publish.not_financial_advice: GoCheckMyNews reports events. It does not editorialize and it does not advise. Nothing here is political advocacy, legal advice, or financial advice.

newsletter.note: Owned audience, highest value, build first. Set endpoint + key to enable.

video.note: Newscast script handoff to the recording workflow.
